extraction.one_line_summary: EVENEPOEL Remco demolished the 70km opening time trial by 43 seconds over ROGLIČ Primož with power 26 seconds ahead at first checkpoint translating to decisive margin.
tactical_patterns: EVENEPOEL Remco established 20-second gap at first checkpoint in long TT, suggesting early sustained power better predictor than final climb pacing.
GEOGHEGAN HART Tao lost only 7 seconds on flat sections but hemorrhaged time on final climb, revealing climbing weakness not TTing deficit.
ALMEIDA João rode 26 seconds slower than EVENEPOEL on flat before climb, indicating his GC deficit rooted in pure power not just climbing.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: 20-second advantage at first checkpoint in 70km TT expanded throughout, demonstrating exceptional sustained threshold power over long distance
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: 43-second loss to EVENEPOEL despite clean ride reveals meaningful TT gap likely to impact final GC battle
rider_signals.VINE Jay: positive: beat MCNULTY Brandon by 2 seconds despite recent knee injury, showing strong early-season form for UAE support role
rider_signals.GEOGHEGAN HART Tao: negative: lost majority of time on final 2km climb after being only 7 seconds down on flats, exposing pure climbing deficit
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: neutral: held third at 29 seconds but was already 26 seconds down before final climb, suggesting threshold issues not climbing problems
rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: neutral: second place 10 seconds down represents expected gap to world-class all-rounder in mixed terrain 70km TT
rider_signals.KÜNG Stefan: neutral: rode solidly but couldn't match EVENEPOEL or GANNA, confirming hierarchy among pure TT specialists on mixed parcours
rider_signals.MCNULTY Brandon: positive: fourth-fastest through first sections with 53+ km/h average before specialists arrived, confirming strong TT development for support rider
